title: bursera filicifolia
intro: Bursera filicifolia, also known as the "Mexican torchwood" or "torchwood", is a plant species in the family Burseraceae, native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its resin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The resin has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, and is said to have numerous health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the resin is often extracted and used in ointments, salves, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
